synthesis.content: The Nigeria Electrification Programme has impacted 7.8 million Nigerian lives with clean energy and provided new or improved electricity services to 1,100,000 households and 11,400 MSMEs. The programme plans to provide new or improved electricity services to 3,244,900 households and 236,986 MSMEs. It also plans to deploy or install 465 MW of renewable energy generation capacity, with 94 MW of PV capacity already installed.

sentences: The Nigeria Electrification Programme has impacted 7.8 million Nigerian lives with clean energy and provided new or improved electricity services to 1,100,000 households and 11,400 MSMEs.
The programme plans to provide new or improved electricity services to 3,244,900 households and 236,986 MSMEs.
It also plans to deploy or install 465 MW of renewable energy generation capacity, with 94 MW of PV capacity already installed.
alternate_contents: The Nigeria Electrification Programme has impacted 7.8 million Nigerian lives with clean energy and provided new or improved electricity services to 1,100,000 households and 11,400 MSMEs. The programme plans to provide new or improved electricity services to 3,244,900 households and 236,986 MSMEs. It also plans to deploy 465 MW of renewable energy generation capacity and has already deployed 94 MW of PV capacity.

The Nigeria Electrification Programme (NEP) has impacted 7.8 million Nigerian lives with clean energy and provided new or improved electricity services to 1,100,000 households and 11,400 MSMEs. The NEP plans to provide new or improved electricity services to 3,244,900 households and 236,986 MSMEs. The programme has deployed or installed 94 MW of PV capacity and plans to deploy or install 465 MW of renewable energy generation capacity.
